LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT AND MOTION TO STAY. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE COURT TAKES JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD. IT DOES NOT APPEAR THAT PLAINTIFF HAS EXHAUSTED THE ADMINISTRATIVE PROCESS AND THAT PLAINTIFF'S REMEDY IS TO TAKE A WRIT FROM THAT PROCEEDING. GUPTA V. STANFORD UNIVERSITY (2004) 124 CAL.APP. 4TH 407. PLAINTIFF'S FUTILITY ARGUMENT MAY BE APPLICABLE TO A WRIT PROCEEDING BUT IT DOES NOT CREATE ANY OF THE REMEDIES PLAINTIFF HAS ALLEGED IN THIS CASE. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

Off Calendar Dec-11-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE TO PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL, for failure to comply with San Francisco Superior Court Early Settlement Program Policies and Procedures Number Four, Administrative fee is ordered off calendar/Case removed from Early Settlement Program. Proceedings not reported. (212)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-11-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT AND MOTION TO STAY.
RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. The Court takes judicial notice of the administrative record. It does not appear that plaintiff has exhausted the administrative process and that plaintiff's remedy is to take a writ from that proceeding. Gupta v. Stanford University (2004) 124 Cal.App. 4th 407. Plaintiff's futility argument may be applicable to a writ proceeding but it does not create any of the remedies plaintiff has alleged in this case. (302/PJM/VC) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-18-2008 Continued To Sep-11-08 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is continued to September 11, 2008. Opposing party to provide courtesy copies of their papers to the Court with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600 (302/PJM)
